creator: andy@neu.net
text:
FreeBSD FBSD_13 13.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 13.0-CURRENT #0 r340490: Fri Nov 16 23:59:50 EST 2018     root@FBSD_13:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/MYKERNEL  amd64 1300003


Reopening this because I believe there is still a bug here.  It is true that installing audio/freedesktop-sound-theme populated the "sound effects" tab with a default theme, however there is still an issue.  Although I can successfully doubleclick on a sound and hear it play through the speakers, the sound will not play as a default event sound through the speakers.

Steps to repeat:

open sound preferences
click sound effects tab
choose default sound theme
check enable button sounds
open a terminal window
press backspace key many times until line is blank and continue to press key

You should be able to hear sound coming from the speakers when continuing to press backspace key.  I do not hear sound from speakers it is coming from the onboard motherboard speaker, the one that beeps when the machine reboots for example.
